sources.text: AWS for Startups - Amazon Web Services (AWS) Skip to main content Filter: All English Contact us AWS Marketplace Support My account Search Filter: All Sign in to console Access Activate AWS Startups Home AWS Credits AI for Startups Offers Migrate More Home AWS Startups Build your startup on AWS The majority of the top 100 startups globally use AI services on AWS. Get up to $200,000 in AWS Activate Credits and expert support to build what's next. Join now Play What’s new The latest from AWS for Startups. Loading Loading Loading Loading Loading Home to over 350,000 startups globally With nearly 20 years of experience gained from supporting hundreds of thousands of startups that have come before, AWS helps founders prove that their world-changing ideas are possible, at any stage of growth or level of funding. Whether your startup is just starting to ideate an MVP or is on track for an IPO, AWS offers a broad range of programs and resources that will support you to reach your next milestone. Innovation powered by AWS Millions of customers, including thousands of the fastest-growing startups, use AWS to build fast, keep costs low and prove what is possible. Expertise from our startup network AWS for Startups comprises former founders and CTOs, venture capitalists, angel investors, and mentors who help today's founders navigate challenges and opportunities. Programs and resources to empower success From your first line of code to your millionth customer, AWS for Startups provides the technical guidance, business mentorship, and go-to-market resources to help you reach your next milestone. Credits to help you test and build Apply to receive up to $200,000 in AWS Activate Credits to offset costs on infrastructure, data services, and AI/ML models. Credits are redeemable on third-party models on Amazon Bedrock. AI startups ready to grow may be eligible for additional credits beyond Activate. Your go-to-market support Co-build, co-market, and co-sell with AWS to accelerate your growth. The AWS Partner Network has more than 140,000 partners from over 200 countries, with 70% headquartered outside of the United States. The technology behind the fastest-growing startups With AWS, founders leverage the latest cloud and AI technologies to experiment and innovate faster. Accelerate your time from idea to revenue with the broadest and deepest set of services on the most secure cloud. AWS Startups: Made in New York, powered by AWS Discover why New York City’s speed and energy is a launchpad for startups building the future with AWS. In this video, five trailblazing founders share their experiences scaling fast-growing companies in America's cultural capital. Play Support at every stage 1 1. Turn your idea into a business You have a big idea that solves a problem, now make it real. Explore articles, videos, and resources built for early-stage founders. Then bring your idea to AWS Startup Advisor and get a personalized support tailored to what you're building. Learn | Start building with AWS Startup Advisor 2 2. Build your minimum viable product Focus on shipping, not infrastructure. AWS Startup Advisor helps you scaffold your architecture and deploy local code to AWS, right from your IDE. Get up to $200,000 in AWS Activate Credits, with additional credits available for AI startups ready to scale. Apply now 3 3. Extend your reach Put your product in front of the customers already buying on AWS. List on AWS Marketplace to reach enterprise buyers and shorten your sales cycle. Learn more | Visit AWS Marketplace 4 4. Reach your next milestone Whether it's your next funding round, your first enterprise customer, or your next hundred users, tap into mentorship, go-to-market resources, and investor connections through the AWS for Startups. Get started Access credits for every stage From first prototype to Series A and beyond. Credits that scale with you. Learn More Build and deploy faster with Kiro credits designed for startups Apply today Startups Fuel your startup’s growth with up to $200,000 in AWS Activate Credits Apply today Did you find what you were looking for today? Let us know so we can improve the quality of the content on our pages Yes No Create an AWS account Learn What Is AWS? What Is Cloud Computing? What Is Agentic AI? Frequently asked questions Expand all Collapse all 01/ What is Microsoft for Startups, and how do I apply? Microsoft for Startups is a program that helps founders overcome common startup challenges by providing access to AI services, expert guidance, and essential technology. The program offers benefits like Startup credits, AI tools, and resources to help you build, scale, and sell your solutions. 02/ What is the Microsoft for Startups portal? The Microsoft for Startups portal supports startups by providing access to various resources, benefits, and programs. Startups can tap into pre-built marketing materials, industry-leading AI services, expert guidance, and essential technology to help build and scale their businesses. 03/ What are Microsoft for Startups technical benefits? Microsoft for Startups provides a range of technical benefits designed to help startups build and scale their businesses. These benefits include: Statup credits: Credits can be applied across eligible Azure services to support your startup from early development through production workloads. Access to AI tools: Startups can leverage industry-leading AI services and tools for software development, enabling them to create innovative and intelligent applications. Technical resources: Startups have access to various resources that assist in building better products, scaling faster, and increasing sales. This includes documentation, guides, and support to help with replication and modernization journeys. Daniel Ni Founder and CEO @ ScraperAPI 1 2 3 Recommended resources Successful Customer Acquisition for Startups For many entrepreneurs attempting to monetize or build a business out of their products, what comes after the initial proof of concept is somewhat of a mystery. Learn more about customer acquisition for startups From Startup to Sustainability: Best Practices for Engineering Teams in Times of Transition A more reliable way to scale where you move fast, and break less. Learn how to structure your business goals to mitigate potential risk, knock down technical debt, and improve processes. Learn more about transitioning from startup to sustainability Startup funding explained: Series A, Series B, Series C Startup funding can get confusing. Outside of bootstrapping a startup with founder funds, many early-stage businesses raise funding from various sources. Learn more about seed funding Frequently asked questions What is DigitalOcean Startups? DigitalOcean Startups helps developers create, launch, and scale their startups. This includes a range of perks to get you started, including 12 months of DigitalOcean credits to easily deploy and scale your infrastructure. Why is DigitalOcean doing this? We want to empower the startup community. We always strive to support and show love to our community. We want it to be easy for you to have access to our resources. Who is eligible for DigitalOcean Startups? How do you select DigitalOcean Startups members? We prioritize AI-native startups. Service-based startups (consulting services, web/app development services, marketing agencies, etc.) are not eligible. New applicants are evaluated using the following criteria: Must have raised $10m or less. Must have a company website, and an email address associated with the company website. Must create a registered DigitalOcean team account with a business/company email (not a personal email account). How do I know if my accelerator/incubator/VC firm has a relationship with the DigitalOcean Startups? Please see our portfolio partner list . You can ask your program manager if you have a partnership with DigitalOcean Startups. I do not see my accelerator listed in the partner section. Can I still apply for the DigitalOcean Startups program? Yes, have your program manager apply to become a DigitalOcean Startups partner h

Most customers save ~40% by switching to Railway. 1 Share your bill from another cloud provider 2 We'll re-price it with Railway's usage-based model 3 Get your savings delivered to your inbox Drop your PDF here Your name Work email How did you hear about us? Select an option Referral/Word of Mouth AI Chat (ChatGPT, Claude, etc) Search (Google, etc) LinkedIn Reddit Twitter / X Youtube Social - Other Github Other What is your current monthly infrastructure spend? Select an option <$250 per month $500 - $999 per month $1,000 - $4,999 per month $5,000 - $9,999 per month $10,000+ per month Unlock my savings Commonly Asked Questions Which plan is right for me? Hobby is for indie hackers and developers to build and deploy personal projects. Pro is for professional developers and their teams shipping to production. Enterprise is for dev teams building and deploying production applications with large replica size, SLA, and/or compliance needs. How do I get started with the free Trial? If you do not already have a Railway account, you can sign up for a free Trial by clicking Login on railway.com. How does the Trial work? When you sign up for the free Trial, you will receive a one-time grant of $5 in credits that you can use

Pricing and plans — PlanetScale Introducing Database Traffic Control™: resource budgets for your Postgres query traffic. Learn more Log in | Get started | Get in touch Platform ▾ | Resources ▾ | Documentation | Pricing | Contact Navigation Platform ▾ | Resources ▾ | Documentation | Pricing | Contact Pricing AI agents can view our pricing on the pricing.md page. Region For best performance, select the same region as your application. Request another region AWS us-east-1 Database engine Postgres NEW The fastest cloud Postgres. Vitess MySQL at hyperscale. Cluster configuration Primary + multi-replica (HA) A primary and two included replica nodes ideal for high-availability workloads. 99.99% SLA . Single node (non-HA) A single primary node suitable for non-high availability use cases. Cluster type High-availability clusters deployed across 3 availability zones . Amazon Elastic Block Storage Network-attached storage volumes with autoscaling storage . The perfect choice for smaller databases with lower I/O requirements. ⚡️ PlanetScale Metal ⚡️ Ultra-fast NVMe drives and unlimited IOPS make high-availability Metal clusters the fastest nodes available in the cloud. Architecture: aarch64 ( 10 ) x86-64 ( 10 ) PS-5 1/16 vCPU · 512 MB memory · 10 GB storage $15 Includes 3 nodes CPU Memory 1× primary database 1/16 vCPU 512 MB 2× replica databases 1/16 vCPU 512 MB 1/16 vCPU 512 MB Total resources 3/16 vCPU 1.5 GB Storage Choose the disk size you would like to provision. GB + Show advanced options Estimated monthly total: $15 Get started PlanetScale Postgres Single node Development and low-traffic production workloads. Starting at $5/month. High Availability 1 primary + 2 replicas across 3 availability zones. Metal High-performance local NVMe storage. Starting at $50/month. Vitess MySQL-compatible with horizontal sharding for massive scale. Runs on network-attached storage or PlanetScale Metal. Plans Base ↓ Enterprise ↓ Learn more in the plans documentation . Also available on the AWS Marketplace and Google Cloud Marketplace . Have questions about Enterprise options, plan features, or pricing? Get in touch Database expertise Augment your team with our database experts, including the majority of the core Vitess maintainers Migration assistance Hands-on, proactive assistance in migrating your workload with no downtime Slack-based support Private shared Slack channel for fast communication More deployment options Multi-tenant, single-tenant, or in your own AWS / GCP account Business support included Enhanced response times from support engineers Architecture consultation Work with our Solutions team to optimize your database design Custom pricing AWS/GCP Marketplace purchase options Additional regions Deploy in any AWS or GCP region with at least 3 availability zones Base Plan The Base plan is what you get when you sign up at PlanetScale . No contract or sales interaction required. Pricing is resource-based, so you can upsize and downsize your instances as needed. PlanetScale Postgres Single node — Starting at $5/month for development and low-traffic workloads Metal — Starting at $50/month with local NVMe storage for high-performance needs High Availability — 1 primary + 2 replicas across 3 availability zones Vitess MySQL-compatible databases with horizontal sharding. Pricing based on cluster size, VTGate configuration, and storage. Metal also available. Pricing factors Pay as you go — Pay month to month based on the price in the calculator above. The main factors that determine your price are: Instance and VTGate sizes Storage amount — First 10 GB included, and then $0.50 per GB per instance. We require a minimum of 3 instances for production branches to ensure high availability. Storage costs are included for Metal instances. Add-ons — Additional development branch hours, SSO, upgraded support For the full list of features and more information, see our plans documentation . Enterprise Our Enterprise plan comes with all of the same platform features you get with the Base plan, but also includes upgraded levels of support and additional deployment options: Bring your own cloud — For enterprise companies or those with strict compliance policies that require your data to remain in your own cloud, we also offer PlanetScale Managed . This allows us to deploy PlanetScale into an AWS or GCP account that you own and we manage. Upgraded support — All Enterprise plans come with Business support and the option to upgrade to Enterprise support. The Enterprise support option is only available on Enterprise plans and gives you access to our team’s database architecture expertise through a shared Slack channel and bi-weekly meetings. Additionally, 15 minute response time for P1 tickets. PlanetScale Managed pricing model If you choose to deploy PlanetScale in your own AWS or GCP account, the required infrastructure is spun up in your account, and you are billed by your cloud provider for those infrastructure costs. This allows you to take advantage of the PlanetScale platform (branching, deploy requests, query monitoring, etc) in your own private environment. On top of the infrastructure fees that you pay directly to your cloud provider, you will pay a percentage of that cost to PlanetScale for the use of PlanetScale, infrastructure management, migration assistance, and ongoing support. For companies with existing AWS or GCP agreements, this option sometimes ends up less expensive than running on PlanetScale’s infrastructure, as you can take advantage of your AWS or GCP discounts for these infrastructure costs. When do I need Enterprise? If you need any of the following, Enterprise may be the best solution for you: Enterprise support — Shared Slack channel with our team and bi-weekly meetings where you can discuss database performance, optimizations, and more. You need hands-on evaluation and migration assistance You need your database deployed in a single-tenant environment You need a PCI DSS certified service provider You need to keep your data in your own AWS or GCP account Any other customizations — Our Enterprise plan offers a lot of flexibility, so if you have a requirement that’s not listed here, it’s best to reach out and we can see how we can help. If you are not involved with a VC, accelerator, or incubator, please apply above on this page. Can I apply if I’m already using MongoDB? Absolutely. You do not have to be new to MongoDB to be eligible for our program. We accept both new and existing MongoDB Atlas users. What if I’ve applied but have not heard back yet? Your essential tools for building, built by founders for founders. 6 months free with Notion AI included For non-paying Notion customers with under 100 employees and are affiliated with one of our select startup partners. 3 months free with Notion AI included For non-paying Notion customers with under 100 employees, a valid business website, and a company domain email address. 1 month free with Notion AI included For non-paying Notion customers with under 10 employees (SMBs) or startups who provided incomplete business information during application. Notion Perks Exclusive software discounts for each part of the founder’s journey Apply to access → Notion Certified Consultants One complimentary session with a consultant to setup your Notion workspace. Apply to access → Join our partner network alongside top VCs and accelerators Offer your network Notion's Business plan free and additional perks! Apply to become a partner → Questions & answers Who is eligible? You must be a non-paying Notion customer with a valid work email (no Gmail, Outlook, etc), have not received any previous Notion promotions, and have fewer than 100 employees. See full terms and conditions . Why is Notion offering a discount for startups? We've seen thousands of startups use Notion as their complete workspace - from using AI to speed up work, capturing meeting notes automatically, managing emails and scheduling, to organizing their startup's knowledge from day one. We want to help more companies lay a strong foundation that can grow with them from the beginning. How do I apply to receive the discount? Fill out this application ! What is the offer worth? Notion’s Business Plan costs $20 per member paid annually. The startup offer is equivalent to a maximum of $12,000 for 100 employees. Why did I receive a 1 month trial instead of 3 or 6 months? You received a 1 month trial because either: You have under 10 employees – SMBs with fewer than 10 employees receive our 1 month trial offer, or You're an SMB or service-based business rather than a startup, or We couldn't fully verify your startup status with the information provided in your application The 3 month and 6 month offers are designed specifically for startups building scalable tech-related products or services. SMBs, service-based businesses, consultancies, agencies, and companies under 10 employees receive the 1 month trial. You may have also received a 1 month trial if one or more of the following apply: Inactive or incomplete website: Your website appears to be a domain parking page, shows errors, or doesn't clearly describe your business Limited business presence: Only personal social media profiles were provided instead of company pages Insufficient information: Not enough details were available to verify your business legitimacy Solo applicant: The application suggests a single individual rather than a company How do I suggest a partner that Notion should work with? Don’t see your investors on our list? There are a few things you can do: Have them email us at [email protected] . Have them fill out the application . I want this offer to be available to my portfolio companies. How can I become a partner? We want to hear from you! Apply to be a partner and we’ll get back to you with more information. We’re especially interested in finding partners outside of major tech hubs. Get access to the best AI-first helpdesk from just $65/month, with 93% off in your first year, 50% off in your second year, and 25% off in your third year. Your Early Stage plan includes the following: 6 Advanced Seats (see all features) 6 Copilot Seats 20 Lite Seats Proactive Support Plus (500 messages sent per month) Plus a free year of Fin AI Agent, including: 300 Fin outcomes per month 15 Fin qualifications per month Pro (unlimited conversations analyzed per month) Your 93% discount applies to usage based costs for email, messages sent (over 500 per month) and additional seats (over 6). The discount does not apply to additional outcomes or qualifications, phone, SMS & WhatsApp, which are charged at list price. See usage based costs . How much does Fin AI Agent cost? Fin AI Agent is billed per outcome, so you only pay for the value it delivers. Your first year includes 300 outcomes and 15 qualifications per month. Fin AI Agent pricing $0.99 per outcome Resolution — No further help is requested after the last AI answer. Procedure handoff — Fin completes a Procedure designed to handoff to a human. Disqualification — Fin disqualifies a lead against your sales criteria. $9.99 per qualification Qualification — Fin qualifies a lead to your sales criteria and routes them to your sales team, CRM, or elsewhere. Learn more about outcomes . What happens after the first year? In your first year, you get a 90% discount and 300 free Fin resolutions per month. In your second year, this decreases to a 50% discount and 150 free Fin resolutions per month, followed by a 25% discount and 75 free Fin resolutions per month in your third year. If the costs become too high after your first year, you can move onto our Essential plan starting at $39 per seat per month. What if we're larger than 15 employees? If your company is not eligible, you can start a trial of our regular plans on our pricing page or connect with our Sales team through Messenger for more options. sources.text: Linear for Startups – Linear Skip to content → Product Resources Customers Pricing Now Contact Docs Open app Log in Sign up Linear for startups Discover the issue tracker that startups love to use. Get up to 6 months free with the Linear Startup Program. Build and scale your product with Linear Linear is the tool of choice for ambitious startups to plan, build, and scale their products. Powerful yet simple to use, it streamlines issues, sprints, and projects. Focus your team on the work that matters and ship faster. The next generation of high-impact startups build with Linear Meet our customers Why startups love Linear Designed to move fast. Just like your startup. Linear is built for teams that want to ship faster. With its minimal design and snappy performance, it delivers a focused experience ideal for small teams with big ambitions. Break work into manageable pieces Create bug reports and manage tasks in a streamlined interface that helps you get stuff done. Build team momentum Use Cycles to focus your team on what work should happen next. Maintain velocity and make meaningful progress. Plan your product Define, track, and ship larger pieces of work from start to finish with projects and roadmaps. Collaborate across tools Linear is integrated with the rest of your tool stack. Connect issues to work in Figma, GitHub, Slack, and more. 45% of YC companies build with Linear 66% of the top AI startups use Linear “Linear helps us be action biased. We’ve been using it since day one.” Linear Startup Program Get started for free with the Linear Startup Program Startups affiliated with a Linear partner can upgrade to Linear’s Basic or Business plans and get up to 6 months off. See all partners Become a partner Get up to Six months free New Linear customer Fewer than 50 employees Affiliated with a Linear partner Apply FAQs Questions and answers What is the Linear Startup Program? The Linear Startup Program is designed to help early-stage startups discover a better, faster way to build and ship great products. Startups affiliated with a partner in Linear’s Startup Program receive up to 6 months of free access to Linear’s Basic and Business plans . Who is eligible? To redeem the offer you must be affiliated with an official Linear partner . The offer is only valid for non-paying Linear users with less than 50 employees. How can I apply? To apply, enter your partner code in the form above or follow the link provided by your partner. You need to be an admin of your Linear workspace and logged into your account to redeem the offer. Is my investor a member of the Linear Startup Program? You can see all participating partners of the Linear Startup Program on this page . If your investor is not part of the program, you can ask them to apply to become a partner . Our company is larger than 50 employees, can I still apply? The Linear Startup Program is designed for early-stage startups. Only companies with fewer than 50 employees are eligible to participate. If your company is larger, please contact our sales team . I’m not affiliated with a Linear partner, can I still apply? Only companies affiliated with an official Linear partner can participate in the program. Linear does, however, offer a generous free plan that includes all major features, allows unlimited workspace members, and 250 issues (plus unlimited archived issues). If your investor is not part of the program and you think they should be, ask them to apply . I’m an investor and would like to offer Linear to my portfolio companies. How can I participate in the program? Please fill out this form to apply to become a Linear partner and we’ll get back to you. I have more questions. Please contact our support team. sources.text: Retool | For Startups Skip to main content Back Solution Audience Resources Use cases Pricing Search ⌘K Sign in Sign in Book a demo Book a demo Start for free Start for free Startups Build a better way to work, for free Stay focused on finding product-market fit. Startups get Retool for free for a year to automate manual tasks with custom internal tools and build customer portals. Value up to $60K. Get started for free Get started for free Why Retool Grow faster with custom internal tools and customer portals Developer-first Building software in Retool is 10x faster than building from scratch. We help developers build tools that speed up internal processes and keep the team as lean as possible. Collaborative We make building internal tools a team sport. Teams can drag-and-drop to define how they want to work, and then it’s a simple task to connect and ship a functional app. Battle-tested Retool is used by fast-growing startups like Brex, Ramp, and Alloy, and scaled companies like Doordash and OpenAI. We're built to grow with you. Partner Deals $200k in partner deals Startups that join our program get access to $200k in partner deals in addition to free Retool—so you can operate your business efficiently from day one. Access the Deal Book Access the Deal Book Frequently Asked Questions Who is eligible for Retool for Startups? Startups that are bootstrapped, angel-funded, debt-funded, pre-seed, seed, or Series A, having raised less than $10 million in total funding, and were founded within the last 10 years. You must be a new customer on the monthly Team or Business plan . The Startup offer cannot be used in conjunction with other discounts offered by sales. How does it work? Eligible startups receive a 100% off coupon for one year, with a value up to $60k. This can be applied to any monthly plan, Team or Business. To claim the offer, first upgrade to your desired monthly plan, then submit an application . You will receive a confirmation email upon acceptance to the program, and the discount will be applied automatically to your first invoice at the end of your first billing cycle. Once claimed, the offer cannot be transferred to a different cloud account or self-hosted deployment. Will the offer expire? The 100% off discount expires after one year. However, you will receive a 25% discount on your paid plan the following year. I'm already a Retool customer. Is my startup eligible? Retool for Startups is for new customers only. Retool customers that have already been on a paid plan for multiple billing cycles are not eligible for the offer. How do I become a Retool for Startups partner? Retool partners with venture capital funds, accelerators, incubators, and startup programs to share offers. If you'd like to add Retool to your deal book or add an offer to our deal book, please contact startups@retool.com . Learn more Learn more Learn more Prev Prev Next Next FAQ Who can apply? Any early-stage founder or startup building with Claude can join the Startups program. To qualify for Claude credits and extras, your company must have received equity funding from institutional investor, been founded within the last four years, and not previously received Anthropic startup credits. You'll need a Claude Console account, a company email, your website, and a short description of what you're building. Do I need VC funding to apply? No. The program is open to any early-stage founder or startup with or without VC backing. If you're backed by an Anthropic partner VC or accelerator, mention it in your application and you may qualify for additional benefits. How do I apply? Fill out the application on this page. It takes about two minutes. You'll need a Claude Console account (the API console, not the Claude app) and a company email address. Can I use credits on AWS Bedrock or Google Vertex AI? No. Claude startup credits apply to the first-party Claude API via the Claude Console. They can't be used on AWS Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, or other third-party platforms. Does the program include higher API rate limits? Yes. Acceptance upgrades you to our highest rate limits , so you can ship to production without throttling while you're on credits. What happens after the program? You keep building. Standard API pricing kicks in automatically with no migration. If you need help optimizing costs as you scale, the Claude for Startups team can help. sources.text: Pricing – Replicate Try HappyHorse 1.0 from Alibaba Replicate glyph Explore Pricing Enterprise Docs Blog Sign in Try for free Menu Explore Pricing Enterprise Docs Blog Sign in Try for free Compare models in the Playground Pricing You only pay for what you use on Replicate. Some models are billed by hardware and time, others by input and output. Public models Thousands of open-source machine learning models have been contributed by our community and more are added every day. We also host a wide variety of proprietary models. Most models are billed by the time they take to run. The price-per-second varies according to the hardware in use. When running or training one of these public models, you only pay for the time it takes to process your request. Some models are billed by input and output. We've included some examples below. You'll find estimates for how much any model will cost you on the model's page. anthropic / claude-3.7-sonnet The most intelligent Claude model and the first hybrid reasoning model on the market (claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219) $0.015 / thousand output tokens $3.00 / million input tokens black-forest-labs / flux-1.1-pro Faster, better FLUX Pro. Text-to-image model with excellent image quality, prompt adherence, and output diversity. $0.04 / output image black-forest-labs / flux-dev A 12 billion parameter rectified flow transformer capable of generating images from text descriptions $0.025 / output image black-forest-labs / flux-schnell The fastest image generation model tailored for local development and personal use $3.00 / thousand output images deepseek-ai / deepseek-r1 A reasoning model trained with reinforcement learning, on par with OpenAI o1 $0.01 / thousand output tokens $3.75 / million input tokens ideogram-ai / ideogram-v3-quality The highest quality Ideogram v3 model. v3 creates images with stunning realism, creative designs, and consistent styles $0.09 / output image recraft-ai / recraft-v3 Recraft V3 (code-named red_panda) is a text-to-image model with the ability to generate long texts, and images in a wide list of styles. As of today, it is SOTA in image generation, proven by the Text-to-Image Benchmark by Artificial Analysis $0.04 / output image wavespeedai / wan-2.1-i2v-480p Accelerated inference for Wan 2.1 14B image to video, a comprehensive and open suite of video foundation models that pushes the boundaries of video generation. $0.09 / second of output video wavespeedai / wan-2.1-i2v-720p Accelerated inference for Wan 2.1 14B image to video with high resolution, a comprehensive and open suite of video foundation models that pushes the boundaries of video generation. $0.25 / second of output video Private models You aren't limited to the public models on Replicate: you can deploy your own custom models using Cog , our open-source tool for packaging machine learning models. Unlike public models, most private models (with the exception of fast booting fine-tunes ) run on dedicated hardware so you don't have to share a queue with anyone else. This means you pay for all the time instances of the model are online: the time they spend setting up; the time they spend idle, waiting for requests; and the time they spend active, processing your requests. If you get a ton of traffic, we automatically scale up and down to handle the demand. For fast booting fine-tunes you'll only be billed for the time the model is active and processing your requests, so you won't pay for idle time like with other private models. Fast booting fine-tunes are labeled as such in the model's version list. Calculate your costs based on your usage needs Startup Grants Program Build intelligent, real-time conversational AI agents into your new product or startup for free with an ElevenLabs Grant. 12 Months free To build, launch & test 33M Characters Valid for 12 months Learn more Compare plans Text to Speech Speech to Text Voice Changer Sound Effects Voice Isolator Music Image & Video Automatic Dubbing Dubbing Studio Voices Studio Productions Free Starter Creator Pro Scale Business Free Free Starter Creator Pro Scale Business Text to Speech (UI) Minutes included Extra minutes Audio quality ~ 10 ~ $0.36 128 kbps, 44.1kHz ~ 30 ~ $0.20 128 kbps, 44.1kHz ~ 121 ~ $0.18 128 kbps, 44.1kHz ~ 600 ~ $0.17 128 & 192 kbps (via Studio & API), 44.1kHz ~ 1,800 ~ $0.17 128 & 192 kbps (via Studio & API), 44.1kHz ~ 6,000 ~ $0.17 128 & 192 kbps (via Studio & API), 44.1kHz FAQs How do text characters and credits work? Each text character you generate consumes credits, with the exact cost depending on the model used. For V1 English, V1 Multilingual, and V2 Multilingual models, 1 text character equals 1 credit. For V2 Flash/Turbo English and V2.5 Flash/Turbo Multilingual models, discounted pricing applies for API usage, costing between 0.5 and 1 credit per character. When do my credits reset, and do unused credits roll over? Your credit allowance resets at the start of each billing cycle, which begins on the day you subscribed. Unused credits can roll over for up to two months as long as you maintain an active paid subscription and do not downgrade or cancel. When your subscription renews, you will receive a new monthly allotment of credits. Is there a limit on how many credits I can use in a single request? Yes. There is a maximum number of credits that can be used in a single generation request. The limit depends on whether you are on the Free tier or a paid subscription. Am I charged for every generation? Credits are charged per generation request, not per download. If you are not satisfied with the output, a limited number of free regenerations may be available as long as the content and certain settings do not change. Whether a regeneration is free is indicated before generating. What happens if I upgrade, downgrade, or cancel my subscription? If you upgrade to a higher plan, any unused credits from your previous plan will roll over into the next billing cycle. If you downgrade or cancel your subscription, your subscription will remain active until the end of the current billing cycle. After that, your account will be downgraded to the Free plan and any unused paid credits will expire. When can I cancel my subscription? You can cancel your subscription at any time. Your subscription will remain active until the end of your current billing cycle, after which it will not renew and your account will be downgraded to the Free plan. How do I check how many credits I have remaining? You can view your remaining credits by logging into the platform and navigating to your subscription page from your profile menu. What kind of payment do you accept? We accept Credit Card, Apple Pay, and Google Pay. Subscriptions are billed on a monthly or yearly basis starting from the day you subscribe. Platform Models Developers Pricing Training Partners Resources Company Log In Get Started Pricing to seamlessly scale from idea to enterprise Start building in seconds, self-serve. Contact us for enterprise deployments with faster speeds, lower costs, and higher rate limits. Get started Contact Us Serverless Inference Get started in seconds with per token pricing, zero setup and no cold starts See Pricing Fine Tuning Customize open models with your own data with minimal setup See Pricing On Demand Deployments Pay per GPU second for faster speeds, higher rate limits, and lower costs at scale See Pricing Serverless Pricing Pay per token, with high rate limits and postpaid billing. Get started with $1 in free credits. Text and Vision Models To review current Serverless Pricing for our most popular models, please visit the Docs page below for specific prices and details on Fast + Priority tiers: • https://docs.fireworks.ai/serverless/pricing Specific details for each individual model can also be found on the model listing page from within the models library . • Additionally, please note that cached input tokens are by default priced at 50% for all text and vision language models, unless otherwise specified. Likewise, batch inference is priced at 50% of our serverless pricing for both input and output tokens. Learn more here . Embeddings Base model parameter count $ / 1M input tokens up to 150M $0.008 150M - 350M $0.016 Qwen3 8B $0.1 Fine Tuning Pricing Serve fine-tuned models for the same price as base models. Supervised & Preference Fine Tuning Priced per 1M training tokens Base Model LoRA SFT LoRA DPO Full Param SFT Full Param DPO Models up to 16B parameters $0.50 $1.00 $1.00 $2.00 Models 16.1B - 80B $3.00 $6.00 $6.00 $12.00 Models 80B - 300B (e.g. Qwen3-235B, gpt-oss-120B) $6.00 $12.00 $12.00 $24.00 Models >300B (e.g. DeepSeek V3, Kimi K2) $10.00 $20.00 $20.00 $40.00 • SFT and DPO prices are shown in $ per 1M training tokens. Training tokens can be estimated with number of tokens in training dataset * number of epochs. Estimation should be multiplied by the average number conversation turns /2 for tuning with intermediate thinking traces. • Please note that when fine-tuning with reasoning traces, including the reasoning_content field for assistant turns will increase the total number of tuned tokens because multi-turn conversations are unrolled into user, assistant, and thinking traces. For further details, please refer to example 2 in the documentation about SFT fine tuning . • Fine-tuning with images (VLM supervised fine-tuning) is also billed per 1M tokens. See this FAQ on calculating image tokens. Reinforcement Fine Tuning Reinforcement fine tuning jobs are priced per GPU hour (billed per second), at the same price as Fireworks on-demand deployment. Please see the section below for details on RFT pricing. On-Demand Pricing Pay per GPU second, with no extra charges for start-up times On demand deployments GPU Type Price ($) per hour H100 80 GB GPU $7.00 H200 141 GB GPU $7.00 B200 180 GB GPU $10.00 B300 288 GB GPU $12.00 • For estimates of per-token prices, see this blog . Results vary by use case, but we often observe improvements like ~250% higher throughput and 50% faster speed on Fireworks compared to open source inference engines. Common questions Contact us Which models can I run on Baseten? You can deploy open source and custom models on Baseten. Start with an off-the-shelf model from our model library . Or deploy any model using Truss , our open source standard for packaging and serving models built in any framework. Which GPUs are available on Baseten? You have control over what GPUs your models use. See our instance type reference for a full list of the GPUs currently available on Baseten. Reach out to us to request additional GPU types. Do you offer free credits to get started? Yes, new Baseten accounts come with credits so you can get to know the UI and experiment with deployments for free. Is Baseten secure? Yes, Baseten is SOC 2 Type II certified and HIPAA compliant. You can read more about our SOC 2 Type II certification here . And you can read more about our HIPAA compliance here . Do I pay for idle time on Baseten? No, you do not pay for idle time – you only pay for the time your model is using compute on Baseten. This includes the time your model is actively deploying, scaling up or down, or making predictions. And you have full control over how your model scales up or down. What level of customer support do you offer? Customer support levels vary by plan. We offer email, in-app chat, Slack, and Zoom support. We also offer dedicated forward-deployed engineering support. Reach out to our team to figure out a customer support level that works for your needs. Do you offer discounts on compute? Yes, discounts on compute can be negotiated as part of our Pro and Enterprise plans. Reach out to our team to learn more. Can I deploy Baseten on my own infrastructure? Yes, you can self-host Baseten in order to manage security and use your own cloud commitments. Talk to our engineers to learn more. Our Developer plan is a great choice for personal projects. You will have 1 free seat with access to LangSmith (5k base traces/month included). The Plus plan is for teams that want to self-serve with moderate usage and collaboration needs. You can purchase unlimited seats with access to LangSmith (10k base traces/month included). You will be able to ship agents with our managed LangSmith Deployment service, with 1 free dev-sized deployment included. The Enterprise plan is for teams that need more advanced administration, security, support, or deployment options. Contact our sales team to learn more. Do you offer a plan for startups? Yes! We offer a Startup Plan for LangSmith, designed for early-stage companies building agentic applications. You’ll get discounted rates and generous free trace allotments to build with confidence from day one. ‍ Apply here to get started with startup pricing. When will I be billed? For the Developer or Plus Plan, seats are billed monthly on the 1st or pro-rated if added mid-month (no credit for removed seats); traces are billed monthly in arrears for your usage. Enterprise plans are invoiced annually upfront. Will you train on the data that I send to LangSmith? No. LangSmith does not use your data to train models. Your traces, prompts, and outputs remain private to your organization. See LangSmith Terms of Service for more information. What is a seat? A seat is a distinct user inside your organization. We consider the total number of users (including invited users) to determine the number of seats to bill. I have a developer account, can I upgrade my account to the plus or enterprise plan? Yes, Developer plan users can easily upgrade to the Plus plan on the Plans and Billing page. For the Enterprise plan, please contact our sales team to discuss your needs. We're here to help! Curious how it all works? Wondering if Llamaindex is the right fit? Our FAQs have the details, but nothing beats seeing it live – let's show you! Contact sales Sign up FAQ 01 Is LlamaParse Open Source? LlamaParse is our commercial platform designed to automate document workflows, including agentic document parsing, extraction and indexing. It provides a generous provision of 10k free credits a month to all new users, but is not open source. Our open source projects, LlamaIndex and Workflows, provide AI builders with the foundational building blocks for creating AI apps. They include both low-level and high-level abstractions that can be deployed in just a few lines of code. 02 How many credits does it cost to parse or extract one page? It depends on the modes or options selected. Basic Parsing: as low as 1 credit Layout-aware agentic parsing with LLMs or VLMs: higher credit cost for greater accuracy LlamaParse allows you to balance cost and accuracy and tweak settings without requiring retraining like traditional document processing solutions. We recommend testing new document types with different settings before configuring persistent pipelines. 03 Does LlamaParse deploy on premises? LlamaParse SaaS is hosted on a secure cloud tenant, with data encrypted in transit and at rest. Cached data is retained only for 48 hours before permanently deletion (done to avoid additional costs as you experiment with various configurations). You have the option to turn caching OFF. For enterprises, we also offer deployment in private VPCs across all cloud providers, ensuring data never leaves your tenant. LlamaParse is available on both the AWS and Microsoft Azure marketplaces. 04 What compliance certifications does LlamaParse have? LlamaParse is certified for: SOC 2 Type II GDPR HIPAA For more details, visit our Trust Center . Fully managed model deployment No shared resources or multi-tenancy overhead Seamless integration with Cohere North Simple startup and self-serve model access Fixed or Flex pricing plans available Cohere Dashboard Speak to sales Frequently asked questions How do I inquire about model customization and private deployment? Cohere supports bespoke model customization for our enterprise customers along with private deployment of all our products. Custom pricing is based on your enterprise needs. Learn more about customization here and private deployment here . How do I get a Trial API key? When an account is created, we automatically create an Trial API key for you. This API key will be available on the dashboard for you to copy, as well as in the dashboard section called “API Keys.” How do I get a Production API key? To get a Production key, you'll need to have Owner privileges (or ask your organization Owner to complete the following steps). Navigate to the Billing and Usage page in your Cohere dashboard. Click on the Get Your Production key button and fill out the Go to Production workflow. What’s the difference between a Trial API key and Production API key? API calls made from a Trial API key are free. However, trial keys are rate limited and are not permitted to be used for production or commercial purposes. API calls made from a Production API key will be charged on a pay-as-you-go basis. Production API keys are designed for production use at scale. Are there any account limitations upon signup? Every account begins as a personal account and only has access to Trial API keys. As a personal account, you will not be able to add other members until you become part of an organization. What’s the difference between an organization and a personal account? At Cohere, an organization is a group of personal accounts that share a singular billing portal. Organizations are not automatically given Production API key access, and a member of the organization must still fill out our application form for production access. Personal accounts cannot share billing information with other accounts. Which model should I pick? Your model selection reflects your relative prioritization of model performance and speed. Larger models offer better performance and are capable of more complex tasks, while smaller models have faster response times. When do I get billed? API calls made from a Trial API key will be free. API calls made from a Production key will be billed on a pay-as-you-go basis. Your bill will be issued at the end of every calendar month or when you reach $250 in outstanding balances. The endpoint I’m using is billed by token. What is a token? Language models understand “tokens” rather than characters or bytes. The number of tokens per word depends on the complexity of the text. Simple text may approach 1 token per word on average, while complex texts may use less common words that require 3-4 tokens per word on average. For more details on tokens, refer to this page . Where do I find pricing for our legacy models? For existing customers: Command pricing is $1.00/1M tokens for input and $2.00/1M tokens for output Command-light pricing is $0.30/1M tokens for input and $0.60/1M tokens for output Command R 03-2024 pricing is $0.50/1M tokens for input and $1.50/1M tokens for output Command R+ 04-2024 pricing is $3.00/1M tokens for input and $15.00/1M tokens for o

Getting Started How-To Articles Subscriptions & Toolkits Semrush One Billing and Account Semrush Toolkits SEO Pricing and Limits SEO Dashboard Site Audit Position Tracking Domain Overview Organic Rankings Top Pages Compare Domains Keyword Gap Backlink Gap Keyword Overview Keyword Magic Tool Keyword Strategy Builder SEO Writing Assistant Topic Research SEO Content Template Backlinks Referring Domains Backlink Audit Sensor SEOquake Semrush Rank On Page SEO Checker Organic Traffic Insights AI Visibility Traffic & Market Local Advertising Content Social AI PR My Reports Folders Agency Partners Platform App Center Integrations Teamwork Data & Metrics The Ultimate SEO Glossary Kompyte API MCP Contact Us Knowledge Base Semrush Toolkits SEO SEO Writing Assistant Checking Your Tone of Voice with the SEO Writing Assistant Tool Checking Your Tone of Voice with the SEO Writing Assistant Tool Try SEO Writing Assistant A brand’s tone of voice is extremely important when it comes to creating content. One of the keys to maintaining your tone of voice is consistency.  A consistent tone of voice builds trust with your audience by communicating with them in the appropriate way and creating a memorable image of your brand. Companies that have a consistent tone of voice through their content marketing channels are more likely to stand out from competitors and build trust among their audiences.  SEO Writing Assistant helps you to make sure that every content piece complies with your overall brand voice on the scale of casualness, neutrality, and formality.  Start your free trial of the Guru SEO Toolkit today! How can this be done? First, install the SEO Writing Assistant app to your Google Docs using Google Workspace Marketplace or add it to your Microsoft Word using Microsoft AppSource . You can also add it as a WordPress plugin or access the tool directly in the  Semrush interface . Next, run the add-on, enter your target keyword(s), and click on the "Get recommendations" button. Click on the "Tone of voice" button to check your copy’s tone of voice score and consistency percentage. The score is a real-time metric, which is powered by a machine-learning algorithm and thousands of human-rated pieces of text. Detect the most casual and most formal sentences that stand out from the overall text. Select the preferred tone of voice goal—casual, neutral or formal—for new content or to update existing text. Next Steps Using the SEO Writing Assistant, you can: Check all your written communication for the tone of voice consistency on the scale of casual, neutral, and formal. Update content pieces that differ from your overall tone (if the entire article’s tone of voice doesn’t match your brand voice). Modify sentences that stand out from your text’s overall tone (if some phrases in the article don’t match with its general tone of voice).  Find out if there’re any filler words or phrases that affect your tone of voice and update the corresponding pieces of content. As a long-term goal, maintain your brand’s tone of voice in each content piece to make all your brand communication consistent. Join now Do you qualify? Choose from three programs with big savings on Amplitude’s AI Analytics Platform. Startup scholarship Get one year of free access to the Amplitude Growth plan—including Analytics, Feature and Web Experimentation, Session Replay, and Activation—plus competitive discounts for life. Criteria: Less than $10M in funding raised Fewer than 20 employees Get started We also offer two more scholarships Tech for Black Founders scholarship Free and unlimited access to the Amplitude Growth plan for as long as the eligibility criteria are met, plus competitive discounts for life. Criteria: Less than $30M in funding raised Fewer than 150 employees Based in the United States Get started Portfolio company discount Save at least 25% or more on the Growth plan for one year if you’re a portfolio company with one of our venture partners. Criteria: Less than $25M in funding Y Combinator, AWS, and Sequoia companies at any funding level Not currently an Amplitude customer Get started What do you get? Premium analytics that won’t eat into your runway First year free Data-driven decision making Amplitude’s all-in-one platform gives you analytics, experimentation, and activation. Use powerful tools to gain clear user insights and find market fit quickly. 15 analytics projects per month with built-in experimentation Key startup features: Session Replay, pathfinder, behavioral cohorts Unlimited feature flags, chart saves, and Activation data Get started Built-in community Get inspiration and confidence Need a hand? Join Amplitude Cohort, a community of 2,400+ peers where you can get answers to questions, learn skills, and level up your analytics practice. Join now Startup friendly Templates and dashboards Save time with pre-built templates and workflows that show you how to spin up your first dashboard, automate reporting, and measure what matters. Get started Helping startups grow “You feel like Amplitude is on your side. They want you to have success and get results, as opposed to just, ‘Hey, here’s your analytics platform.’” Alex Tew CEO, Calm Additional programs Save at every stage As a trusted partner of leading accelerators and global VCs, Amplitude is proud to help startups scale and succeed with trusted insights for each step. Portfolio company discount > Tech for Black Founders scholarship > Join hundreds of startups who save on AI analytics Get your Startup Scholarship Frequently asked questions How do I get support from Amplitude? For tech support, implementation, and feature questions, you can access the Amplitude Support Forum . If you need help with non-technical issues, join our community Slack space . Both support channels are available to startups as part of Amplitude Cohort , our free support community of 2,400+ peers and experts. How many applicants get approved for Amplitude Startup Scholarships? We approve over 96% of scholarship applicants. You’re qualified for an Amplitude scholarship if you are a: Startup with <20 full-time employees and <$10M in funding Black-led startup with <150 employees, <$30M in funding, and based in the United States Portfolio company with an Amplitude venture partner, and not a current customer Y Combinator, Sequoia, and AWS ecosystem companies are eligible for the Amplitude Scholarship program regardless of their funding or headcount. What are the limits to the Amplitude Growth plan? The Amplitude Growth plan has a data limit of 200,000 monthly tracked users (MTUs). Each time you track a user interaction inside one of your analytics projects, that’s an MTU. For early-stage startups, 200K MTUs is typically more than enough. You can also choose to measure data use by event volume (100M events/month) instead of MTUs if it helps extend your data usage. To explore your options, contact us at scholarship@amplitude.com . sources.text: Global Hiring for Startups | Save $1,200 Per Team Member with Oyster Skip to main content Skip to main content 3 months of free Oyster See limited time offer → Oyster Home Products Global Employment Employer of Record Hire and pay global full-timers Global Contractors Hire and pay global contractors Salary Insights Access salary and benefits insights Add-ons People Partner Services White-glove HR advisory for your global workforce Employee Benefits Attract and retain talent with competitive benefits Visa Sponsorship Sponsor visas and relocate employees with ease US PEO Outsource your US payroll, benefits, and HR Platform Integrations Connect Oyster to your HR tech stack API Automate your workflows with our API Oyster Embedded Enable your customers to hire globally Oyster Partners Let's build the future of work together Availability Hire in United Kingdom United States Canada Philippines Mexico India Brazil Spain See all Why Why Oyster Compare Oyster See how Oyster differs from other platforms Switch EORs Bring your team to Oyster Convert Contractors Transition contractors to full-timers Expand Globally Test new talent markets Exceptional Support Model Be guided by a team of experts See Case Studies Hear from our customers Resources Hiring Global Hiring Guides HR best practices in 50+ countries Employment Cost Calculator Calculate employment taxes and costs Global Employment Tools Free tools for global HR Learning Library See the latest blogs and news Events Browse all events and webinars Podcasts Hear the latest episode Pricing Log In Sign Up Book a Demo Oyster Home GLOBAL HIRING FOR STARTUPS The EOR built for startups hiring globally Oyster's employer of record (EOR) lets startups hire full-time team members in 120+ countries - no entities, no legal headaches. Save up to $1,200 per person with Oyster’s startup discount. Book a Demo Meet Oyster Get a Demo Leave your details to get a call from one of our Oyster experts and discover the best available price to fit your company needs. Trusted by early-stage startups building global teams Hiring should help you grow, not slow you down Why startups choose Oyster EOR Growing startups need to move fast, but international hiring often means months of legal setup, compliance headaches, and hefty fees. Oyster eliminates the friction—hire anywhere in days, not months. Book a Consultation Hire in 180+ countries Hire full-time employees globally without establishing local entities or navigating foreign regulations. Built for small teams Get expert guidance at every step. From contracts to compliance, we handle the complexity so you can focus on growth. Stay compliant, globally We manage local employment laws, tax requirements, and regulatory changes across every market you enter. Startup-friendly pricing Save thousands per hire with pricing built for early-stage companies. What you see is what you pay—no surprise fees. How Oyster Works Employer of Record, made easy Employment laws handled for you Avoid legal risks with built-in HR and payroll compliance in 180+ countries. No legal entity required. Expert local support Lean on people who understand employment laws, tax rules, and hiring norms in every country you’re expanding to. Hire faster, without switching tools Onboard full-time employees and contractors in days—not months. Oyster integrates with your existing HR stack. Retain your best people, wherever they live Offer localized benefits and meaningful support that help your team feel valued—and stay longer. See how Oyster works How Embeddable saves thousands on international expansion We’ve used Oyster to onboard 10 team members in countries like Germany, Finland, Portugal, France, Austria, and Ireland. Oyster enables us to onboard team members quickly and seamlessly, with good information and guidance throughout Vilte Dewar Business Operations Manager, Embeddable Watch video Trusted by global teams everywhere Verified reviews from companies hiring globally Verified User in Computer Software “ Really like Oyster's ability to provide knowledge across the complexities of hiring in so many countries saving us a tremendous amount of time and possible legal issues.” Verified User in Financial Services “ The team is incredibly responsive to any questions or requests I have. The employee dashboard is intuitive and user-friendly, so most of my questions are easily answered just by visiting the homepage.” Verified User in Computer Software “ How easy it is to approach Oyster and their dedication to solve my questions and challenges in a very effective and fast manner. They take feedback seriously and act upon it!” Verified User in International Payments “ Oyster provided clear and detailed documentation highlighting all the important work functions. The attachment of drafts of certain documents such as payslips was very helpful in understanding what to expect and how your net income will look after taxes in a foreign country.” GET STARTED IN 3 STEPS Ready to hire through an EOR? With Oyster, you’re just a few clicks away from hiring the best talent across the world. Get started today and see how quickly you can make your global employment goals a reality. Go to Oyster step 1 Sign up for your free Oyster account Explore all platform features at no cost - including the Employment Cost Calculator to plan your global hiring budget. step 2 Build your team with confidence Onboard team members, run global payroll, and offer competitive benefits across countries—all with country-specific expertise built in. step 2 Get expert help as you need it Get responsive support from local experts who understand the nuances of global employment, from compliant contracts to benefits administration. Compliant agreements in 180+ countries Benefits and payroll in 120+ currencies Time and expense tracking Global relocation contracts FAQs for Startups What is Oyster's EOR for Startups? A discounted program for early-stage companies to hire, pay, and manage global talent using Oyster’s employer of record platform. No entities required. Is Oyster right for my startup? Yes, if you need to hire globally without the legal headaches. Oyster is perfect for startups that want to access talent beyond expensive local markets, scale quickly without setting up entities, and compete for top performers with a professional hiring experience. We're built for pre-seed through Series A companies and bootstrapped startups under 50 employees. How does the startup discount work? Save up to $1,200 per team member annually for your first 12 months. No minimum hires required. If you've raised pre-seed, seed, or Series A funding—or you're bootstrapped with under 50 employees—you qualify. Just request the discount when you sign up. What’s included in the $1,200 savings? Everything you need for global employment: compliant contracts, local payroll in 120+ currencies, competitive benefits, tax compliance, and ongoing HR support. Instead of paying lawyers to set up entities or hiring dedicated HR staff, you get the full infrastructure at startup-friendly rates. How long is the discount valid? The discount is valid for 12 months from the date of hire. What countries can I expand to? 120+ countries for full-time employees. Hire anywhere from emerging markets with great talent and lower costs to specialized hubs worldwide—all without setting up legal entities or navigating local employment laws. Save up to $1,200 per hire Hiring globally doesn’t have to be complicated—or expensive for startups Oyster EOR helps startups hire in 120+ countries without the legal lift or high fees. Let’s get your next team member onboarded. personalized demo Meet with a Greenhouse expert to learn how we help you cut costs, reduce risk and hire better faster. With our industry-leading hiring platform you can: Make the best hires faster every time, with solutions from sourcing to interviewing to onboarding Navigate unrelenting internal and external change with a platform that will scale and flex with you, thoughtfully integrating new technology like AI into the workflows you use day to day Take back control of your pipeline, allowing you to sort and filter candidates that best align with your job description while avoiding the risks of fraud, spam and misrepresentation Create an objective, inclusive hiring process with structured hiring, recruiter/HR alignment and bias mitigation Thank you FAQ How does Greenhouse pricing work? Greenhouse pricing is customized based on your company’s hiring needs. You choose a plan – Core, Plus or Pro – based on the level of structure, automation and governance your hiring organization requires. A Greenhouse expert can recommend the right plan and provide custom pricing based on your hiring needs. You can chat with an expert here . What’s included in Core vs. Plus vs. Pro? Each plan builds on the one before it: Core includes essential hiring tools to create structure, consistency and a strong candidate experience. Plus adds automation, advanced configuration and deeper reporting to support hiring. Pro includes enterprise-grade governance, security, analytics and extensibility for complex, global hiring operations. Start with what you need now and scale into more advanced capabilities as your hiring evolves. ‍ How much does Greenhouse cost? The cost of Greenhouse is influenced by the plan you choose (Core, Plus or Pro), your hiring volume and organizational complexity, and the features and capabilities required to support your workflows . ‍ Can I upgrade plans later? Yes. You can upgrade your plan at any time. Greenhouse plans are built to support your organization as your hiring needs and organizational complexity change. Questions on pricing Is there a free trial I can try? Yes! When you sign up, you automatically start out on the 15-day free trial. You’ll have access to almost all of Homerun's ATS and HR features during the trial. After your trial expires you can pick our Starter, Core or Plus plan, add your payment method and you’re all set! Do Homerun fees include VAT? Using Homerun in The Netherlands For Homerun users in The Netherlands, VAT is charged on top of Homerun fees at the Dutch VAT rate, currently 21% Using Homerun in Europe outside The Netherlands Homerun does not charge VAT on Homerun fees to customers located in European countries outside The Netherlands. Instead, you will need to self-account for VAT in your local VAT return under the reverse charge scheme at the prevailing VAT rate in your local country. Your accountant will be able to assist you with VAT reporting through the reverse charge scheme. Using Homerun outside Europe ‍ Homerun does not charge VAT on to customers located outside European countries. What payment methods can I use? We accept all major credit cards and debit cards. You can easily add a credit card or make use of the SEPA payment method. How do annual contracts work? With an annual contract, you commit to using Homerun for 12 months. You can pay per month or paid annually. Can I cancel anytime? The Starter, Core, and Plus plan all run on a yearly commitment - just let us know at least 30 days before your renewal date and we won't renew you for another year. non-profits 20% discount for non-profits Doing great things for the world? With Oyster, you can find refugee talent and hire them compliantly—at no or discounted costs. Learn More Oyster for startups We love seeing startups embrace global employment and distributed work. Fuel your growth by building a global, talented team and save up to $1,200 on EOR subscription fees for all team members. Learn More FAQs Does Oyster charge additional fees for setup, onboarding, or offboarding? Oyster does not add extra charges for essentials like setup and onboarding, talking to HR experts, or processing terminations —it’s all included in your subscription fee. Oyster requires a refundable deposit for EOR Team Members to initiate the engagement and to ensure on-time payments to your team. What currency can I pay Oyster in? Oyster accepts payments in USD, EUR, GBP, or CAD. Oyster accepts both direct debit and wire transfers as payment methods. You can easily set up direct debit or auto-withdrawal and pay Oyster in one click through the platform. How does Oyster handle currency conversions? Oyster charges a fee for currency conversions but the fee applies only if you pay us in a currency different from the contract currency. Click here to learn more. Is there a minimum number of team members required to use Oyster? No, you can use Oyster to engage as many global candidates as you need. The Oyster subscription fee is charged per active Team Member. How does annual billing work for Employer of Record? Oyster offers a seat-based, annual billing option for the Employer of Record product. Using this option, you purchase annual seats at a reduced fee. Your seats can be used for new or existing EOR Team Members. When a seat becomes empty, you can reuse it for new roles or backfills at no additional cost. Learn more here . Can I sign up for the Oyster platform for free? Yes, it’s totally free to create an Oyster account, explore the platform, and access global HR tools and resources. The Oyster fee will only kick in once you’ve engaged a contractor, an EO

Startup School is a free online course on how to start a startup. Sign up Sign in Get advice based on 15 years of YC's knowledge, stay accountable for weekly progress, and find your co-founder. How Startup School works Get the essential advice for ambitious startup founders, taught by Y Combinator partners and industry leaders. Go at your own pace. It will take approximately 7 weeks if you spend 1-2 hours/week. Who is Startup School for? It's for anyone at the early stages of building a startup, turning a side project into a company, or anyone curious to learn about becoming a founder. The YC Curriculum Learn how to build a top startup - from the same people who helped Airbnb, Doordash, Stripe, Reddit, and Coinbase get started.
